name: Michigan State University Extension Service-Washtenaw
description_short: Michigan State University Extension Service provides free, unbiased financial and housing education through certified counselors, webinars, and self-paced learning resources.
description_long:
Michigan State University Extension Service–Washtenaw is part of MSU Extension, a university-based educational program designed to help Michigan residents build financial health and achieve homeownership. Established as a public service mission, the organization operates through the College of Agriculture & Natural Resources at Michigan State University and serves diverse audiences regardless of financial status.

The service offers a comprehensive range of free educational resources including online and in-person classes, one-on-one counseling with certified housing and money management counselors, self-paced learning modules, webinars, and practical guides. Their MI Money Health platform covers foreclosure prevention, home buying preparation, money management, retirement planning, and specialized programs like Rent Smart (tenant education) and estate planning discussions. They host regular events including homeownership seminars, mortgage foreclosure education sessions, and youth-focused programs like the Smart Money Missions Goosechase Game for ages 13-19.

What distinguishes this organization is its university affiliation, certified counselor staff, and commitment to delivering trusted, unbiased education at no cost. As a government-funded Extension Service, they have no profit motive and focus specifically on demystifying financial concepts for everyday Michigan residents. Their impact is documented through published annual reports measuring outcomes for participants in financial and homeownership education programs.

The main limitation is that this is primarily an educational and counseling resource rather than a direct financial assistance provider. While they offer foreclosure prevention guidance and homeownership education, they do not provide loans, credit repair, debt settlement, or direct financial products. Their services are geographically tied to Michigan and Washtenaw County specifically.
pros: Certified housing and money management counselors providing expert, unbiased guidance
Completely free educational resources with no hidden fees or product sales
Multiple learning formats: webinars, in-person classes, articles, videos, and self-paced modules
Specialized programs addressing specific needs like foreclosure prevention, rental preparation, and estate planning
University-backed credibility with published impact reports documenting program outcomes
Youth-focused programming including interactive financial literacy games for ages 13-19
Regular scheduled events with flexible timing (virtual and in-person options)
cons: Limited to Michigan residents only; geographically restricted service area
Educational resource only—cannot provide direct financial assistance, loans, or debt relief services
Dependent on university funding and Extension Service availability; limited personalized one-on-one counseling capacity
No direct mortgage lending or housing financing options; educational guidance on buying process only
Website navigation issues (referenced 404 error page) may create accessibility barriers for some users
best_for: Michigan homebuyers and renters seeking free education on property purchase and rental processes
Families facing foreclosure looking for prevention education and options counseling
Youth ages 13-19 interested in building foundational money management skills
Michigan residents seeking unbiased financial planning guidance without sales pressure
diagnosis: Best for Michigan residents seeking free, unbiased financial education from a credible university source, particularly those preparing to buy a home, facing foreclosure, or wanting to improve money management skills. Primary caveat: This is an educational counseling service only—it does not provide loans, credit repair, debt settlement, or direct financial assistance, so consumers needing immediate financial products should look elsewhere.
services: Free homeownership education webinars and in-person seminars
Foreclosure prevention counseling and options education (keep, sell, or let foreclose)
Money management and personal finance education
Rent Smart program (tenant financial preparation and lease education)
Estate planning family discussions (Who Gets Grandma's Yellow Pie Plate?)
Self-paced online learning modules and articles
Youth financial literacy programs (Smart Money Missions Goosechase Game)
Custom community presentations on emerging financial issues
Resource library with publications, videos, and downloadable guides
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
address: 705 N Zeeb Rd, Ann Arbor, MI 48103
